title: Garlicky Smoky Argentinian Chicken Empanadas
description: A delightful fusion of smoky, garlicky flavors and crunchy pecans in a classic Argentinian empanada.
instructions: Roll out puff pastry and cut into circles or use cookie cutter to create shapes.
In a pan, heat pecan oil over medium heat. Add smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add chicken breast to the pan and cook until browned, breaking into small pieces as it cooks.
In a separate pan, heat beef broth over medium heat. Add beaten egg and stir constantly until egg is cooked through.
Add cooked chicken mixture to the egg mixture and stir to combine. Set aside.
Place a spoonful of the chicken mixture onto one half of each pastry circle. Sprinkle with shredded cheese.
Fold the other half of the pastry circle over the filling to form a half-moon shape. Press edges to seal.
Brush tops with a little water and use a fork to crimp edges.
Place empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes.
